summary: postinstall exfiltrates /etc/passwd and installer username to attacker-controlled Pipedream endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle hook that runs python3 to read /etc/passwd and POST its contents over plain HTTP to a subdomain of m.pipedream.net keyed to the installer's username via $(whoami). This fires automatically on `npm install` with no user interaction. The behavior — reading a non-package-owned host file, encoding the installer's identity into the request hostname, and transmitting to a non-first-party request-bin service — is host reconnaissance and data exfiltration. There is no legitimate purpose for a package's install hook to read /etc/passwd and send it to a third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
